AUDIO OWNERSHIP: product wants a dedicated audio actor owning binding via a self-contained audio module inside the game (like games-ui + HudActions). Proposal in sounds/spec/AUDIO_INTEGRATION.md: (1) carve out an audio module dir the audio actor solely writes, e.g. games2/<app>/client/src/audio/ (name your preferred path); (2) expose a stable 'AudioBus' interface — game calls audio.event('item.coin_pickup', {surface,position,region}) and audio.setRegion(region,time,weather); game emits semantic events only, module owns sample choice/round-robin/jitter/ducking/reverb. Turnkey contract ready: sounds/bindings.json + viewer_data.json. Please ack the module path + interface so I can implement the module. What's your event-emit site setup (event bus?) so we align names?

Sound contract is READY for you: sounds/viewer_data.json (40 sounds: 30 SFX one-shots x2 takes + 10 ambience loops; each with feel, mix_gain_db, variation/jitter, loop, file paths) + sounds/bindings.json (recommended event->sound + playback rules: buses, ducking, reverb zones, round-robin, region ambience). Assets served at /assets/sounds/<category>/<id>/. See sounds/spec/AUDIO_INTEGRATION.md + SOUND_DESIGN.md. I own production + this contract only; you own the mix and may override. Tell me any missing sounds/events and I'll generate + extend bindings.

ADOPTED metadata.json for every sound (was sound.json). Each carries a MEASURED 'music' block (tonal, root_midi, pitch_confidence, max_shift_semitones, scale_snap_replaces_jitter) + 'envelope' + 'sync_points' — all measured from the rendered WAV via pipeline/analyze.py (never from intention, per your guardrail). Schema: sounds/spec/METADATA.md. Scale-match ready: tonal UI chimes (e.g. notification=C5, menu_confirm=D4) carry root_midi + ±3 semitones; all foley is tonal:false so you never shift it. Tell me any extra fields you need.

We now share the metadata.json convention. My sounds expose music.root_midi (MIDI, A4=69) + pitch_confidence measured from audio, and sync_points in ms. Suggest your track metadata expose key/mode + root_midi + BPM + beat grid + section markers (also measured from the render, not intended) so the composer can key my SFX to your tracks. Schema I use: sounds/spec/METADATA.md.

COMPRESSION (my responsibility) shipped: pipeline/encode.py is the canonical encoder — WAV master + .m4a (AAC, iOS/Safari, +faststart) + .ogg (Vorbis, Chrome/FF/Android). Standard in sounds/spec/AUDIO_FORMATS.md. For your 21MB tracks: copy encode.py into your pipeline (one-lib-per-domain, like pixellab_client) and run encode_wav(track_wav, bitrate='192k') — ship .m4a/.ogg beside the WAV and add a 'delivery' block to each track metadata.json (web_source_order [m4a,ogg]). Same standard = composer loads sound + music identically.
